ddls:
CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric */,
  "configuration_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the configuration ID */,
  "size" INTEGER /* File size in bytes */,
  "frames" INTEGER /* Total number of frames */,
  "fps" REAL /* Frames per second (FPS) */,
  "kbs" REAL /* Kilobits per second (Kbps) */,
  "time" REAL /* Encoding time in seconds */,
  "quality_score" REAL /* Quality score (e.g., PSNR, SSIM) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., media engineer, data analyst, admin) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Creation timestamp */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Update timestamp */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "configuration_id" INTEGER /* ID of the configuration being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the configuration */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the configuration was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, modify) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configuration_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "configuration_id" INTEGER /* ID of the configuration */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* Version number */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date of the version */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the changes made in this version */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User ID who created the version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configuration_versions_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configuration_performance_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance history entry */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* ID of the performance metric */,
  "configuration_id" INTEGER /* ID of the configuration */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* ID of the version */,
  "size" INTEGER /* File size in bytes */,
  "frames" INTEGER /* Total number of frames */,
  "fps" REAL /* Frames per second (FPS) */,
  "kbs" REAL /* Kilobits per second (Kbps) */,
  "time" REAL /* Encoding time in seconds */,
  "quality_score" REAL /* Quality score (e.g., PSNR, SSIM) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configuration_performance_history_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES configuration_versions ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configuration_performance_history_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES performance_metrics ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configuration_comparisons" (
  "comparison_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comparison */,
  "configuration_id_1" INTEGER /* ID of the first configuration being compared */,
  "configuration_id_2" INTEGER /* ID of the second configuration being compared */,
  "comparison_date" TEXT /* Date of the comparison */,
  "performance_difference" TEXT /* Performance difference (e.g., file size reduction, time improvement) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the comparison results */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("comparison_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configuration_tags" (
  "tag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tag */,
  "configuration_id" INTEGER /* ID of the configuration */,
  "tag_name" TEXT /* Name of the tag (e.g., high-quality, low-latency) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tag_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configuration_comments" (
  "comment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comment */,
  "configuration_id" INTEGER /* ID of the configuration */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the comment */,
  "comment" TEXT /* Comment text */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Creation timestamp */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("comment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configuration_comments_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configuration_ratings" (
  "rating_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rating */,
  "configuration_id" INTEGER /* ID of the configuration */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the rating */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Rating value (e.g., 1-5) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Creation timestamp */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rating_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configuration_ratings_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
column_names: Unique identifier for each performance metric
Role of the user (e.g., media engineer, data analyst, admin)
Unique identifier for each access event
ID of the configuration being accessed
ID of the user accessing the configuration
Date when the configuration was accessed
